title: EDR auth + authz
description: Operator dashboard for the wave-1 auth surface: OIDC + break-glass logins, authz chokepoint denies, bootstrap-token issuance, and audit-write health. Imported into SigNoz via Dashboards -> Import. The queries below use the structured-log shape the EDR audit async writer emits (see server/identity/internal/audit/async.go) plus the OTel span attributes declared in server/attrkeys/keys.go. SigNoz's import dialog re-numbers panels; the layout block is a starting hint only.

panels.title: Authz deny rate by reason
panels.description: Count of audit rows whose action starts with authz. and whose payload.decision = deny, grouped by payload.reason. A baseline of low-volume reauth_required denies is healthy (operators triggering the freshness gate); a sudden spike on role_missing or actor_anonymous is worth chasing. Source: structured slog stream emitted by the async audit writer.

panels.title: Login outcomes (OIDC + break-glass)
panels.description: Stack of successful and failed logins across both auth surfaces. Reads directly from audit-row structured logs: OIDC emits auth.oidc.success / auth.oidc.failure / auth.oidc.callback.error; break-glass emits auth.breakglass.success / auth.breakglass.failure. A flat success line plus a sustained failure line means brute-force or a misconfig; a failure spike with no success spike often means the IdP is down.

panels.title: Bootstrap-token issuance
panels.description: Count of auth.breakglass.bootstrap audit rows. First-boot redemption issues exactly one row per banner re-print, so a steady non-zero rate means the operator never redeemed; a one-off spike outside a deploy window means a manual SQL insert (recovery path) or a fresh boot. Compare against deploy timeline before paging.

panels.title: Audit-write failures (dropped / store error)
panels.description: The async audit writer emits a slog WARN when it cannot enqueue (queue_full / writer_stopped) or when the global drain deadline expires (drain_deadline_exceeded), and a separate WARN when an INSERT into audit_events fails. Any non-zero rate here is a hard alert: the audit log is the spec's append-only invariant and dropping rows breaks it. Cross-reference dropped count via the AsyncWriter.Dropped() counter on the /healthz endpoint.
